Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: Computes cumulative distribution \(G(t)\), density \(g(t)\), hazard \(h(t) = g(t)/(1 - G(t))\) parametric family defined half-life, time exponent, shape.  single function generates temporal phase shapes used multiphase hazard models.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
code: hzr_decompos(time, t_half, nu, m)

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: time Numeric vector times (must > 0). t_half Half-life: time \(G(t_{1/2}) = 0.5\). Must > 0. nu Time exponent controlling rate dynamics. SAS early: NU.  SAS late: relates GAMMA/ETA. m Shape exponent controlling distributional form. SAS early: M.  SAS late: relates GAMMA/ALPHA.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: named list three numeric vectors, length time: G Cumulative distribution \(G(t) \[0, 1]\). g Density \(g(t) = dG/dt \ge 0\).  "early" phase temporal pattern. h Hazard \(h(t) = g(t)/(1 - G(t)) \ge 0\).  "late" phase temporal pattern.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: original C code used separate parameterizations early (DELTA, RHO/THALF, NU, M) late (TAU, GAMMA, ALPHA, ETA) phases.  collapse onto three parameters .  See hzr_argument_mapping() full translation table.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: Six cases defined signs nu m: combination m < 0 nu < 0 undefined raises error.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: Blackstone EH, Naftel DC, Turner Jr. decomposition time-varying hazard phases, incorporating separate stream concomitant information. J Stat Assoc. 1986;81(395):615–624. doi:10.1080/01621459.1986.10478314 Rajeswaran J, Blackstone EH, Ehrlinger J, Li L, Ishwaran H, Parides MK. Probability atrial fibrillation ablation: Using parametric nonlinear temporal decomposition mixed effects model. Stat Methods Med Res. 2018;27(1):126–141. doi:10.1177/0962280215623583

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1)  # Case 1: standard sigmoidal (m > 0, nu > 0) d1 <- hzr_decompos(t_grid, t_half = 3, nu = 2, m = 1) plot(t_grid, d1$G, type = "l", main = "CDF (m=1, nu=2)")   # Case 1L: Weibull-like (m = 0, nu > 0) d1L <- hzr_decompos(t_grid, t_half = 3, nu = 2, m = 0)  # Case 2: heavy-tailed (m < 0, nu > 0) d2 <- hzr_decompos(t_grid, t_half = 3, nu = 2, m = -1)

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: Computes cumulative intensity \(G_3(t)\) derivative \(g_3(t) = dG_3/dt\) late-phase parametric family used original Blackstone C/SAS HAZARD code.  Unlike hzr_decompos() (computes early-phase G1 — bounded CDF), function can produce unbounded values, making suitable modelling increasing late risk.

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
code: hzr_decompos_g3(time, tau, gamma, alpha, eta)

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: time Numeric vector times (must > 0). tau Positive scalar scale parameter. gamma Positive scalar time exponent. alpha Non-negative scalar shape parameter (0 selects limiting case). eta Positive scalar outer exponent.

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: named list two numeric vectors, length time: G3 Cumulative intensity \(G_3(t) \ge 0\) (may exceed 1). g3 Derivative \(g_3(t) = dG_3/dt \ge 0\).

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: \(\alpha > 0\): $$G_3(t) = \bigl(\bigl((t/\tau)^\gamma + 1\bigr)^{1/\alpha}                       - 1\bigr)^\eta$$ \(\alpha = 0\) (limiting exponential case): $$G_3(t) = \bigl(\exp\bigl((t/\tau)^\gamma\bigr) - 1\bigr)^\eta$$

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: Blackstone EH, Naftel DC, Turner Jr. decomposition time-varying hazard phases, incorporating separate stream concomitant information. J Stat Assoc. 1986;81(395):615–624. doi:10.1080/01621459.1986.10478314

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1)  # Weibull-like: alpha = 1 gives G3(t) = (t/tau)^(gamma*eta) d <- hzr_decompos_g3(t_grid, tau = 1, gamma = 3, alpha = 1, eta = 1) plot(t_grid, d$G3, type = "l", main = "G3: power law (gamma=3)")   # General case with alpha > 0 d2 <- hzr_decompos_g3(t_grid, tau = 2, gamma = 2, alpha = 0.5, eta = 1)

what: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: Creates hzr_phase object describing one term multiphase additive cumulative hazard model.  Pass list phases argument hazard() dist = "multiphase".

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
code: hzr_phase(   type = c("cdf", "hazard", "constant", "g3"),   t_half = 1,   nu = 1,   m = 0,   tau = 1,   gamma = 1,   alpha = 1,   eta = 1,   formula = NULL,   fixed = character(0) )

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: type Character; phase type: "cdf", "hazard", "g3", "constant". t_half Positive scalar; initial half-life (time \(G(t_{1/2}) = 0.5\)).  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases. SAS early: THALF/RHO. nu Numeric scalar; initial time exponent.  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases.  SAS early: NU. m Numeric scalar; initial shape exponent.  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases.  SAS early: M. tau Positive scalar; scale parameter "g3" phases. SAS late: TAU. gamma Positive scalar; time exponent "g3" phases. SAS late: GAMMA. alpha Non-negative scalar; shape parameter "g3" phases. alpha > 0, generic G3 formula used; alpha = 0 gives exponential limiting case.  SAS late: ALPHA. eta Positive scalar; outer exponent "g3" phases. SAS late: ETA. formula Optional one-sided formula (e.g. ~ age + nyha) phase-specific covariates.  NULL (default), phase inherits global formula hazard(). fixed Character vector naming shape parameters hold fixed optimization.  Valid names "cdf"/"hazard": "t_half", "nu", "m", "shapes" (shorthand three).  Valid names "g3": "tau", "gamma", "alpha", "eta", "shapes" (shorthand four).  Fixed parameters held starting values; mu (covariates) estimated.  Ignored "constant" phases. mirrors SAS/C HAZARD workflow shapes typically fixed scale parameters estimated.

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: S3 object class "hzr_phase" elements: type Phase type string. t_half Initial half-life (cdf/hazard phases). nu Initial time exponent (cdf/hazard phases). m Initial shape exponent (cdf/hazard phases). tau Scale parameter (g3 phases). gamma Time exponent (g3 phases). alpha Shape parameter (g3 phases). eta Outer exponent (g3 phases). formula Phase-specific formula NULL. fixed Character vector fixed parameter names (may empty).

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: "cdf" Early risk resolves time. \(\Phi(t) = G(t)\), bounded \([0, 1]\). SAS equivalent: Early / G1 phase. "hazard" Late aging risk accumulates. \(\Phi(t) = -\log(1 - G(t))\), monotone increasing. SAS equivalent: Late / G3 phase. "constant" Flat background hazard rate. \(\Phi(t) = t\). shape parameters estimated. SAS equivalent: Constant / G2 phase.

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
code: # Classic 3-phase Blackstone pattern early <- hzr_phase("cdf",      t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0) const <- hzr_phase("constant") late  <- hzr_phase("g3", tau = 1, gamma = 3, alpha = 1, eta = 1)  # Fix all shapes (C/SAS-style: only estimate mu) early_fixed <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                           fixed = "shapes") late_fixed  <- hzr_phase("g3", tau = 1, gamma = 3, alpha = 1, eta = 1,                           fixed = "shapes")  # Fix only some parameters early_partial <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                             fixed = c("nu", "m"))  # Phase with specific covariates early_cov <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                         formula = ~ age + shock)  # Use in hazard(): # hazard(Surv(time, status) ~ age, data = dat, #        dist = "multiphase", #        phases = list(early = early, constant = const, late = late))

what: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: Computes \(\Phi_j(t)\) one phase additive model \(H(t|x) = \sum_j \mu_j(x) \Phi_j(t)\).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
code: hzr_phase_cumhaz(   time,   t_half = 1,   nu = 1,   m = 0,   type = c("cdf", "hazard", "constant") )

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: time Numeric vector times (> 0). t_half Half-life parameter (> 0). nu Time exponent. m Shape parameter. type Phase type: "cdf" (early — uses \(G(t)\)), "hazard" (late — uses cumulative hazard \(h(t)\)), "constant" (flat rate — \(\Phi = t\)).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: Numeric vector cumulative hazard contributions \(\Phi(t)\), length time.

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: "cdf": \(\Phi(t) = G(t)\).  Bounded \([0, 1]\).  Models early risk resolves time. "hazard": \(\Phi(t) = -\log(1 - G(t))\).  Monotone increasing. Models late aging risk.  cumulative hazard derived hazard function \(h(t)\), since \(\int_0^t h(s)\,ds = -\log(1 - G(t))\). "constant": \(\Phi(t) = t\).  Ignores t_half, nu, m. Equivalent exponential (constant hazard rate).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1) phi_early <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, t_half = 2, nu = 2, m = 0,                                type = "cdf") phi_late  <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, t_half = 5, nu = 1, m = 0,                                type = "hazard") phi_const <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, type = "constant")

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: time Numeric vector times (> 0). t_half Half-life parameter (> 0). nu Time exponent. m Shape parameter. type Phase type: "cdf" (early — uses \(G(t)\)), "hazard" (late — uses cumulative hazard \(h(t)\)), "constant" (flat rate — \(\Phi = t\)).

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: Numeric vector instantaneous hazard contributions \(\phi(t)\), length time.

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: "cdf": \(\phi(t) = g(t)\) (density). "hazard": \(\phi(t) = h(t) = g(t)/(1-G(t))\). "constant": \(\phi(t) = 1\).

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1) phi_early <- hzr_phase_hazard(t_grid, t_half = 2, nu = 2, m = 0,                                type = "cdf") phi_late  <- hzr_phase_hazard(t_grid, t_half = 5, nu = 1, m = 0,                                type = "hazard")

title: OMC: Open Mitral Commissurotomy — omc
text: Data 339 patients underwent open mitral commissurotomy University Alabama Birmingham. Contains repeated thromboembolic events (3 per patient) left censoring, exercising interval censoring likelihood.

what: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
text: Produces prediction outputs hazard object. Supports multiple prediction types including linear predictor, hazard, survival probability, cumulative hazard.

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
code: # S3 method for class 'hazard' predict(   object,   newdata = NULL,   type = c("hazard", "linear_predictor", "survival", "cumulative_hazard"),   decompose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
text: object hazard object. newdata Optional matrix data frame predictors. types requiring time (e.g., "survival", "cumulative_hazard"), newdata include time column, time taken fitted object's data. type Prediction type: "linear_predictor": Linear predictor η = x·β (available multiphase) "hazard": Hazard scale exp(η) (available multiphase) "survival": Survival probability S(t|x) = exp(-H(t|x)) "cumulative_hazard": Cumulative hazard H(t|x) event times decompose Logical; TRUE model multiphase, return data frame per-phase cumulative hazard contributions alongside total.  Ignored single-distribution models.  Default FALSE. ... Unused; included S3 compatibility.

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
text: Numeric vector predictions.

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
text: Weibull models survival cumulative_hazard predictions: Cumulative hazard: H(t|x) = (μ·t)^ν · exp(η) Survival: S(t|x) = exp(-H(t|x)) Time values must positive finite. newdata contains time column, used; otherwise, time vector fitted object used. models fit time_windows, predictions type = "linear_predictor" "hazard" also require time values (via newdata$time fitted-time fallback) window-specific coefficients can selected.

type = "cumulative_hazard") #> [1] 0.2244716 0.5138491 1.5356737  # ── Patient-specific survival curves ───────────────────────────────── set.seed(1001) n   <- 180 dat <- data.frame(   time   = rexp(n, rate = 0.35) + 0.05,   status = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.6),   age    = rnorm(n, mean = 62, sd = 11),   nyha   = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   shock  = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.18) ) fit2 <- hazard(   survival::Surv(time, status) ~ age + nyha + shock,   data  = dat,   theta = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, beta1 = 0, beta2 = 0, beta3 = 0),   dist  = "weibull", fit = TRUE )  new_patients <- data.frame(   time = c(0.5, 1.5, 3.0),   age  = c(50, 65, 75),   nyha = c(1, 3, 4),   shock = c(0, 0, 1) ) # Compute predictions from the clean covariate frame before adding columns surv   <- predict(fit2, newdata = new_patients, type = "survival") cumhaz <- predict(fit2, newdata = new_patients, type = "cumulative_hazard") new_patients$survival          <- surv new_patients$cumulative_hazard <- cumhaz new_patients #>   time age nyha shock  survival cumulative_hazard #> 1  0.5  50    1     0 0.9494097        0.05191482 #> 2  1.5  65    3     0 0.7743185        0.25577202 #> 3  3.0  75    4     1 0.5046535        0.68388317  # \donttest{ # ── Grouped survival curves ─────────────────────────────────────── if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(ggplot2)    t_grid <- seq(0.05, max(dat$time), length.out = 80)   profiles <- data.frame(     label = c("Low risk (age 50, NYHA I)",               "High risk (age 75, NYHA IV)"),     age   = c(50, 75),     nyha  = c(1, 4),     shock = c(0, 1)   )    curve_list <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(profiles)), function(i) {     nd <- data.frame(       time  = t_grid,       age   = profiles$age[i],       nyha  = profiles$nyha[i],       shock = profiles$shock[i]     )     nd$survival <- predict(fit2, newdata = nd, type = "survival") * 100     nd$profile  <- profiles$label[i]     nd   })   curve_df <- do.call(rbind, curve_list)    ggplot(curve_df, aes(time, survival, colour = profile)) +     geom_line() +     scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +     labs(x = "Months after surgery",          y = "Freedom from death (%)",          title = "Predicted survival by risk profile",          colour = NULL) +     theme_minimal() }  # }  # \donttest{ # ── Multiphase predictions with decomposition title: Summarize a hazard model — summary.hazard
code: # ── Single-phase Weibull summary ──────────────────────────────────── fit <- hazard(time = rexp(30, 0.5), status = rep(1L, 30),               theta = c(0.3, 1.0), dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE) summary(fit) #> hazard model summary #>   observations: 30  #>   predictors:   0  #>   dist:         weibull  #>   engine:       native-r-m2  #>   converged:    TRUE  #>   log-lik:      -51.3802  #>   evaluations: fn=14, gr=6 #>  #> Coefficients: #>     estimate  std_error   z_stat      p_value #> mu 0.4751371 0.08497102 5.591755 2.247861e-08 #> nu 1.0748592 0.15491372 6.938438 3.964594e-12  # \donttest{ # ── Multiphase model summary ──────────────────────────────────────── set.seed(42) n   <- 200 dat <- data.frame(   time   = rexp(n, rate = 0.25) + 0.01,   status = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.65) ) fit_mp <- hazard(   survival::Surv(time, status) ~ 1,   data   = dat,   dist   = "multiphase",   phases = list(     early = hzr_phase("cdf",      t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0),     late  = hzr_phase("cdf",      t_half = 5,   nu = 1, m = 0)   ),   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(n_starts = 3, maxit = 500) ) summary(fit_mp) #> Multiphase hazard model (2 phases) #>   observations: 200  #>   predictors:   0  #>   dist:         multiphase  #>   phase 1:      early - cdf (early risk) #>   phase 2:      late - cdf (late risk) #>   engine:       native-r-m2  #>   converged:    TRUE  #>   log-lik:      -389.632  #>   evaluations: fn=234, gr=40 #>  #> Coefficients (internal scale): #>  #>   Phase: early (cdf) #>                   estimate std_error z_stat p_value #>   log_mu     -4.432043e+00        NA     NA      NA #>   log_t_half -7.237202e-01        NA     NA      NA #>   nu          1.913460e-03        NA     NA      NA #>   m           8.278897e-06        NA     NA      NA #>  #>   Phase: late (cdf) #>                estimate std_error z_stat p_value #>   log_mu      2.4487765        NA     NA      NA #>   log_t_half  4.2496498        NA     NA      NA #>   nu          1.6407281        NA     NA      NA #>   m          -0.7935705        NA     NA      NA # }
